name: Espresso Cheesecake
recipeInstructions:
Recipe by: Bon Appetit, August 1989
Chocolate coffee bean candies -- optional
For Crust:
Preheat oven to 325°F. Wrap foil around the bottom and up the sides of a 9-inch springform pan. Mix all crust ingredients in a medium bowl until well blended. Press crust mixture into the prepared pan. Bake for 10 minutes. Cool on a rack.
For Filling:
Boil coffee in a small saucepan until reduced to 1/4 cup. Let cool completely. Using an electric mixer, beat cream cheese, sugar, cornstarch, and salt in a large bowl until smooth. Beat in eggs and yolks one at a time, stopping occasionally to scrape down the sides of the bowl. Mix in espresso, half and half, lemon juice, coffee liqueur, grated lemon peel, and vanilla. Pour filling into crust.
Let stand 15 minutes at room temperature. Preheat oven to 325°F. Insert a toothpick into any air bubbles that appear on the surface of the cheesecake. Place cheesecake in a large baking pan. Add enough cold water to the pan to come 3/4 inch up the sides of the cheesecake. Bake until the cake edges are set but the center still moves slightly when the pan is gently shaken, approximately 1-1/2 hours.
Remove cheesecake from water bath. Discard foil. Transfer cheesecake to a rack and let cool. Cover and refrigerate until well chilled (can be prepared up to 2 days ahead). Spoon whipped cream into a pastry bag fitted with a medium star tip. Pipe rosettes of cream around the top edge of the cheesecake. Top each rosette with a coffee bean and serve.
